items.title: Caterpillar Announces Leadership Transition in Construction Industries
items.description: Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE: CAT) today announced that Tony Fassino, group president of Construction Industries, has elected to retire effective May 31, 2026, following 30 years with the company. Caterpillar veteran Rod Shurman has been appointed group president of Construction Industries effective Feb. 1, 2026, at which time Fassino will become group president, retired, for the remainder of the transition.

items.title: Assessing Caterpillar: Insights From 18 Financial Analysts
JP Morgan Maintains Overweight on Caterpillar, Raises Price Target to $740
items.description: JP Morgan  analyst Tami Zakaria   maintains Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) with a Overweight and raises the price target from $730 to $740.

items.title: Beyond AI: Industrial Sector Investment Themes To Watch
items.description: AI investment has dominated the industrial space. TD Asset Management's Juliana Faircloth discusses new themes emerging which may continue to propel the sector forward.

items.title: Caterpillar Inc. (CAT): A Bull Case Theory
items.description: We came across a bullish thesis on Caterpillar Inc. on Chip Stock Investor’s YouTube channel. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on CAT. Caterpillar Inc.’s share was trading at $636.53 as of January 13th. CAT’s trailing and forward P/E were 32.35 and 28.49, respectively according to Yahoo Finance.
